_comment: Universal pulse: active probes of every critical HME element. Failure writes one-line entry to log/hme-errors.log via LIFESAVER so the next turn sees it immediately — not 48 minutes later. Streak-based to suppress transient flakes.

process_probes.name: worker
process_probes._why: 48-min GIL hang was invisible because worker stayed ALIVE while one thread pegged CPU. Kill the invisibility: alert when a tracked process holds >=90% CPU for >=90s.

process_probes.name: proxy
process_probes._why: Proxy is node; a runaway event loop (or middleware stuck in a tight sync loop) shows as sustained high CPU.

file_freshness_probes.name: hook_latency_log
file_freshness_probes._why: Every hook writes to this log on exit via _safety.sh's EXIT trap. If it hasn't been touched in 15 minutes during an active session, hooks aren't firing — something upstream (hook JSON, plugin cache, _proxy_bridge) is broken.

file_freshness_probes.name: hme_activity
file_freshness_probes._why: Activity bridge writes here on every turn_complete. Stale-for-30min means turn-end hooks stopped firing.

hook_latency_probes.name: all_hooks_p95
hook_latency_probes._why: Every hook self-logs wall-time to this JSONL on exit (via _safety.sh EXIT trap). p95 > 500ms over the last 10 min (min 10 samples) means turn-end and tool-call flow is visibly slowing. Previously captured but never alerted on.
hook_latency_probes._threshold_notes: Per-hook overrides — `stop` legitimately runs ~600-700ms (autocommit + detectors run_all + /audit curl + lifesaver scan); 900ms allows normal variance without false alarms. sessionstart includes RAG-engine cold-start context-meter + holograph snapshot; 2.5s budget is realistic. userpromptsubmit includes LIFESAVER scan + onboarding checks; 700ms.
